zh.lifeSummary: 當Abigail Rose於1544年出生在英國英格蘭戴文艾可姆斯時，她的父親Thomas Rose29歲，母親Elizabeth Wordle21歲。她於1563年5月15日與John Collins II在英國素福爾可布拉福德結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於1590年11月在英國英格蘭艾可布萊崔享年46歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭艾可布萊崔。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Abigail Rose于1544年出生在英国英格兰戴文艾可姆斯时，她的父亲Thomas Rose29岁，母亲Elizabeth Wordle21岁。她于1563年5月15日与John Collins II在英国素福爾可布拉福德结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和1个女儿的父母。她去世于1590年11月在英国英格兰艾可布萊崔享年46岁，埋葬在英国英格兰艾可布萊崔。

nameMeanings.name: Rose
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English, Scottish, French, Walloon, Danish, and German: from the name of the flower, Middle English, Old French, Middle High German rose (from Latin rosa), in various applications. In part, it is a topographic name for someone who lived at a place where wild roses grew, or a topographic or habitational name referring to a house bearing the sign of the rose. It is also found, especially in Europe, as a nickname for a man with a ‘rosy’ complexion (compare 4 below). In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed cognates and similar-sounding names from other languages, e.g. Hungarian Rózsa (see Rozsa ), Slovak Róža and Czech Roza . Compare 6 below and French Larose 2.
English: from the Middle English female personal name Rohese, Roese, later Rose, Royse (ancient Germanic Hrodohaidis, Rothaid, composed of the elements hrōd ‘fame, renown’ + haid(is) ‘kind, sort’).
English and Scottish: variant of   Ross  .

variants.name: Rosenbach
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Hans.
German: habitational name from any of numerous places so named (see   Rose   and   Bach  ).

variants.name: Roser
meanings: German: topographic name for someone who lived at a place where wild roses grew (see Rose 1), with the suffix -er denoting an inhabitant, or an occupational name for a rose grower, or a nickname for a lover of roses.
German (Röser): habitational name for someone from any of the places called Rös, Roes, or Rösa in Bavaria, Rhineland, and Saxony.
German (Röser): variant of Rösser (see Rosser 3).

variants.name: Roseman
meanings: English (Middlesex): from the Middle English female personal name Rosamunde (ancient Germanic Rosmund, from hros ‘horse’ + mund ‘protection’). The personal name was widely understood in the Middle Ages as Latin rosa munda ‘pure rose’ and therefore as an epithet of the Virgin Mary.
Jewish (Ashkenazic): artificial name or name adopted by the husband of a woman bearing the Yiddish personal name Royze (see Rose 9).
Americanized form of German Rosemann or Rosmann (see Rossmann ).

variants.name: Roos
meanings: Dutch (also De Roos): topographic or habitational name referring to a house distinguished by the sign of a rose, or a metonymic occupational name for someone who grew roses, from roos ‘rose’.
Dutch: from the female personal name Rosa (from Latin rosa ‘rose’).
Dutch: nickname from roos ‘erysipelas’, an infection which causes reddening of the skin and scalp, applied presumably to someone with a ruddy complexion.

variants.name: Reisen
meanings: Jewish (Ashkenazic): possibly an artificial name from northeastern Yiddish reyzn ‘roses’ (standard Yiddish royzn); see Rose 9.
German: unexplained.

variants.name: Rosado
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Luis, Juan, Carlos, Rafael, Angel, Manuel, Francisco, Ramon, Miguel, Jorge, Pedro.
Spanish: nickname for someone with a notably pink and white complexion, from Spanish rosado ‘pink’, Late Latin rosatus, a derivative of rosa (see Rose ).

variants.name: Roesel
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Erwin, Otto.
German (Rösel): diminutive of Rose .

variants.name: Rosette
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Carlos, Jose, Arturo, Cirilo, Consuelo, Cristina, Eloy, Eufrosina, Felipe, Jesus, Jorge, Manuel.

French: from a pet form of the female personal name   Rose   6 ‘rose’, or a habitational name from any of several places called (La) Rosette, e.g. in Yonne, denoting a place where roses grow. This surname is also found in the West Indies and Mauritius.
Americanized form of Norwegian   Roseth  .

variants.name: Rozsa
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Hungarian Sandor, Tibor, Zoltan, Zsuzsanna, Balint, Gabor, Gyula, Imre, Istvan, Katalin, Laszlo, Miklos.  German Armin, Erwin, Theodor.

Hungarian (Rózsa): from the female personal name Rózsa, from the vocabulary word rózsa ‘rose’. In some cases the surname might derive from an old male personal name of the same form. Compare Roja 2 and Rose 1.

variants.name: Roese
meanings: German (Röse):
variant of   Rose  .
possibly also a habitational name from a place called Rösa near Bitterfeld.

nameMeanings.name: Abigail
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Biblical name, meaning ‘father of exaltation’ in Hebrew, borne by one of King David's wives, who had earlier been married to Nabal (1 Samuel 25:3), and by the mother of Absalom's captain Amasa (2 Samuel 1:25). The name first came into general use in Britain in the 16th century, under Puritan influence. It was a common name in literature for a lady's maid, for example in  Beaumont  and  Fletcher's  play The Scornful Lady (  1616 ). The biblical Abigail refers to herself as ‘thy servant’ in addressing King David. In Ireland this name has traditionally been used as an Anglicized form of Gobnat , although the reasons for this are not clear. It was popular in the 17th century, especially among Puritans and Nonconformists, and has again enjoyed considerable favour since the 1990s.

en.lifeSummary: When Abigail Rose was born in 1544, in Exmouth, Devon, England, United Kingdom, her father, Thomas Rose, was 29 and her mother, Elizabeth Wordle, was 21. She married John Collins II on 15 May 1563, in Bramford, Suffolk, England. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 1 daughter. She died in November 1590, in Braintree, Essex, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 46, and was buried in Braintree, Essex, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Abigail Rose est née en 1544, à Exmouth, Devon, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Thomas Rose, avait 29 ans et sa mère, Elizabeth Wordle, avait 21 ans. Elle a épousé John Collins II le 15 mai 1563, à Bramford, Suffolk, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée en novembre 1590, à Braintree, Essex, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 46 ans, et a été enterrée à Braintree, Essex, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Abigail Rose 1544 in Exmouth, Devon, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Thomas Rose 29 und ihre Mutter Elizabeth Wordle 21. Sie hat am 15. Mai 1563 in Bramford, Suffolk, England John Collins II geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Sie starb im November 1590 im Alter von 46 Jahren in Braintree, Essex, England, United Kingdom und wurde in Braintree, Essex, England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Abigail Rose è nata il nel 1544 nel Exmouth, Devon, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, sua padre, Thomas Rose, aveva 29 anni e sua madre, Elizabeth Wordle, aveva 21 anni. Lei sposato John Collins II il 15 maggio 1563, nel Bramford, Suffolk, England. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 1 figlia. Morì nel novembre 1590, nel Braintree, Essex, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 46 anni, e fu sepolta a Braintree, Essex, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Abigail Roseが1544年にイギリスイギリスデボンエクスモウスで生まれた時，父Thomas Roseは29歳，母Elizabeth Wordleは21歳であった。1563年5月15日にイギリススフォルクブラムフォードでJohn Collins IIと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼女は1590年11月にイギリスイギリスエセックスブレイントレで46歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスエセックスブレイントレにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Abigail Rose(이)가 1544년 영국 잉글랜드 데폰 스모우스에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Thomas Rose(은)는 29세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Elizabeth Wordle(은)는 21세였다. 그녀는 1563년 5월 15일 영국 수폴크 브램퍼드에서 John Collins II(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1590년 11월 영국 잉글랜드 에세스 브레이트리에서 46세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 에세스 브레이트리에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Abigail Rose nasceu em 1544, em Exmouth, Devon, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, Thomas Rose, tinha 29 anos e sua mãe, Elizabeth Wordle, tinha 21 anos. Ela casou-se com John Collins II em 15 de maio de 1563, em Bramford, Suffolk, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 1 filha. Ela faleceu em novembro de 1590, em Braintree, Essex, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 46 anos, e foi sepultada em Braintree, Essex, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Abigail Rose родилась в 1544 г. в местности эксмоут, девон, Англия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени Thomas Rose было 29 лет, а матери, которую звали Elizabeth Wordle, 21. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени John Collins II 15 мая 1563 г. в местности брамфорд, суффолк, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 1 дочь. Она умерла ноябрь 1590 г. в местности бреинтри, эссекс, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 46 лет и была похоронена в местности бреинтри, эссекс, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Abigail Rose nació en 1544, en Exmouth, Devon, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, Thomas Rose, tenía 29 años y su mamá, Elizabeth Wordle, tenía 21 años. Se casó con John Collins II el 15 de mayo de 1563, en Bramford, Suffolk, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 1 hija. Murió en noviembre de 1590, en Braintree, Essex, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 46 años, y fue sepultada en Braintree, Essex, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
